methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Apis Cor is a seed-stage company in Housing & Construction that has raised $5.5M to date. The sector comp universe is limited, consisting of two identified private peers (ICON and BotBuilt) with no public comparables available, reflecting the nascent state of this technical domain.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised ($5.5M) as a proxy for seed-stage value, a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier to the next funding round yields an implied valuation of $14M. A peer-median approach could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within Housing & Construction—the narrow peer set fails to meet the minimum threshold of three datapoints needed for statistical validity.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $12M to $16M, with a median point of $14M. This range reflects application of a single method and should be interpreted with caution given the methodological constraints. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer multiples or comparable rounds in the sector, which limits validation of the step-up multiplier itself. Investors should supplement this analysis with bottom-up unit economics, market sizing, and technical feasibility assessment before finalizing a valuation for this stage.
